question_text: A book rests on a table. A student draws a free body diagram showing four forces: weight (down), normal force (up), friction (left), and applied force (right). The book is stationary. Which statement about Newton's third law pairs is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: Weight and normal force are a Newton's third law pair because they are equal and opposite

options.label: B
options.text: Weight is paired with the Earth's gravitational pull on the book; normal force is paired with the book pushing down on the table

options.label: C
options.text: Applied force and friction are a Newton's third law pair

options.label: D
options.text: All four forces shown must be Newton's third law pairs for the book to be stationary

explanation: Newton's third law pairs act on different objects. Weight (Earth pulling on book) pairs with the book pulling on Earth. The normal force (table pushing on book) pairs with the book pushing on the table. Equal and opposite forces on the same object aren't third law pairs—they're balanced forces.
